SEASONED STAR POWER 

Virat Kohli remains the ultimate X factor — a right handed run machine who lives for the big moments and thrives under spotlight pressure. Against CSK, his numbers underline his aura: 1,146 runs in 34 matches, a rock solid strike rate of 128, and a highest score of 90* that screams clutch performance. Virat doesn’t just score runs , he controls tempo, sets the vibe, and drags the game into his orbit. Whether it’s anchoring an innings or flipping the switch with back to back boundaries, his presence alone raises intensity levels. Calm face, killer intent , Kohli is still him, and every time he walks in, the momentum meter tilts his way.

Shivam Dube, on the other hand, is pure late order chaos — a left handed finisher built for impact overs and instant damage. In just 6 matches, he’s hammered 215 runs at a blistering strike rate of 160, with a towering 95 showcasing his six hitting range. Dube doesn’t wait around — if it’s in his arc, it’s gone. Against RCB, he’s been the definition of a momentum hack, turning decent totals into scary ones in a flash. No warm up, no hesitation — just clean swings and big vibes. When Dube sets up at the crease, bowlers know the equation can go sideways real quick.

 

RED Vs YELLOW : PLAYER SHOWDOWNS

Virat Kohli vs Noor Ahmed is a clash soaked in tension, temperament, and tactical intrigue. The master right hander against a fearless left arm chinaman — this duel has produced sparks already. Kohli has scored 63 runs off 44 balls, but Noor has landed crucial blows with 2 dismissals, proving he isn’t overawed by reputation. It’s wrist spin versus willow, guile versus greatness. Kohli looks to impose himself with his trademark intensity and strike rotation, while Noor attacks with drift, dip, and daring, dragging the contest into that uneasy middle overs battlefield where one moment can flip the game on its head. Every delivery feels like a chess move calculated, aggressive, and absolutely unforgiving.

Sanju Samson vs Bhuvneshwar Kumar brings together red hot form and seasoned mastery. Samson, the flamboyant right hand opener and reigning ICC T20 World Cup 2026 Player of the Tournament, has scored 62 runs from 42 balls in this matchup alone. But standing in his way is Bhuvneshwar Kumar — the evergreen swing king, deadly with the new ball and ice cold at the death. With 2 dismissals to his name against Samson, Bhuvi’s ability to nip it early and deceive late turns this into a high stakes thriller. It’s fearless shot making versus relentless accuracy , experience and nerve — a rivalry where one perfect over or one mistimed stroke could define the night.

 

 

 

HEAD TO HEAD IN IPL 

 

MATCH PREVIEW

M Chinnaswamy Stadium sets the stage for a heavyweight clash as defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru square off against five-time champions Chennai Super Kings in a contest dripping with pedigree and pressure. When red meets yellow in Bengaluru, the stakes rarely need selling, and this one arrives with momentum on one side and urgency on the other.

RCB enter this encounter high on confidence after a commanding start to their campaign. That opening win showcased depth and dominance, with the batting unit clicking in unison. Devdutt Padikkal, Virat Kohli and Rajat Patidar all found runs with authority, setting the tone early. A brief gap since that win has only sharpened the anticipation, and RCB will be keen to pick up exactly where they left off, especially on a surface that rewards fearless strokeplay.

Chennai Super Kings, however, walk in desperate for lift off. Two games in and yet to register a win, CSK find themselves searching for rhythm amid growing injury concerns. The absence of MS Dhoni for nearly a month and Dewald Brevis still on the recovery trail has stretched their resources. Still, there were positive signs in the last outing as Mhatre delivered a fluent 73 at a healthy strike rate, well backed by Shivam Dube’s late flourish. Those sparks will need to turn into a sustained flame.

History, hunger and home advantage collide under Bengaluru lights. One side looks to cement authority, the other to rescue momentum. As noise swells and nerves tighten, the question looms large. Can CSK script a comeback or will the Defending champions continue their charge?

SPOTLIGHT PERFORMERS

Abhishek Sharma has enjoyed notable success against Lucknow Super Giants, scoring 160 runs in 5 innings at an average of 40 and a blistering strike rate of 229, including two half-centuries. Meanwhile, Aiden Markram has been in exceptional form in 2026, amassing 652 runs in 17 innings at an average of 47 and a strike rate of 162, registering five fifties and a century — six scores of fifty or more — underlining his consistency and impact with the bat.
 

 

PLAYER BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR 

A few key individual battles could play a decisive role in shaping the contest. Ishan Kishan has enjoyed a dominant run against Mohammad Shami, putting together 57 runs from 51 balls at a strike rate of 112, with Shami yet to get him out. On the other hand, the matchup between Rishabh Pant and Jaydev Unadkat has been more evenly contested — Pant has scored 49 runs off 31 balls at a strike rate of 158, but Unadkat has managed to dismiss him twice.
 




HEAD TO HEAD:

While Lucknow Super Giants hold the overall edge in this fixture, recent trends tilt slightly in favour of Sunrisers Hyderabad, who have won the last meeting and two of the previous three encounters.

 

MATCH PREVIEW 

The first game of Sunday’s double-header shifts focus to the Orange Army as Sunrisers Hyderabad return to familiar surroundings for their first home fixture of the season at Uppal. They take on the Lucknow Super Giants in what promises to be an intriguing contest.

Sunrisers Hyderabad head into this clash with renewed confidence after a spirited win in Kolkata. In doing so, they became the first side this season to successfully defend a total, bouncing back strongly from their opening defeat. With a formidable top order featuring Abhishek Sharma, Travis Head, and skipper Ishan Kishan, the hosts will be eager to build momentum and kickstart a winning streak in front of their home crowd.

Lucknow Super Giants, on the other hand, have questions to address following a disappointing start with the bat in their opening loss, where they were restricted to 141. A key talking point remains the batting order, particularly the decision to have captain Rishabh Pant open alongside Mitchell Marsh. For Lucknow to challenge effectively, their overseas core of Marsh, Aiden Markram, and Nicholas Pooran will need to step up. Their pace attack, however, showed promise in the previous outing and could play a crucial role once again.

With both teams looking to assert themselves early in the tournament, this contest sets up as a compelling start to Sunday’s action.
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Mohammed Shami (Player of the Match):
"I just tried to do my job and I am happy because after losing the first game it was important for us to win and regain some momentum. If you truly love cricket and want to play at a high level then working hard is non negotiable and fitness helps you execute your skills better. Playing domestic cricket helped me find my rhythm again and get into the right flow. I had played at this ground last year and knew that slower balls could be effective here so that was part of the plan. It feels good when the hard work comes together and contributes to a team win."

Played with fire ???????? pic.twitter.com/sToiZU8CGc

— Lucknow Super Giants (@LucknowIPL) April 5, 2026



Rishabh Pant (Lucknow Super Giants Captain):

"It is a big positive to stay till the end and finish the game because that is what wins you matches. The bowlers deserve a lot of credit as they set the game up beautifully with contributions from Shami, Avesh, Rathi and Siddharth. We know there is never a perfect match, so as a management we have to stay critical while also appreciating the effort. The focus is always on execution and learning from every game. Personally I feel well prepared and I want my bat to do the talking because the people around me know how hard I work and that is what really matters."
 

Ishan Kishan (Sunrisers Hyderabad Captain):
"It was a very competitive game and although the total was short, our bowlers did an outstanding job by sticking to the plans and hitting the right areas, with great support from the fielders as well. Losing early wickets in the powerplay always puts pressure on the rest of the batting unit and that is something we need to address because it makes the chase simpler for the opposition. Credit must go to Klaasen and Nitish for batting exactly as the situation demanded and taking the score as deep as possible. We did plan to get Rishabh’s wicket early but he played smartly, nudged singles and built the partnership that was required, which is what good chasers do. Bowling Harshal for those overs was a calculated call and he executed really well, but when margins are small, a couple of boundaries can change everything, and despite the loss taking it to the final over gives us positives to carry forward."


Lucknow Super Giants completed a composed chase at Hyderabad to defeat Sunrisers Hyderabad by five wickets, after controlling the game through discipline with the ball and a calm finish with the bat.

Sunrisers were put under pressure early after opting to bat. Mohammed Shami produced a superb opening spell, finishing with 2 for 9 from four overs and reducing the hosts to 8 for 2 inside three overs. At 26 for 4 in the eighth over, Hyderabad were struggling before Heinrich Klaasen and Nitish Kumar Reddy mounted a strong recovery. Klaasen struck a fluent 62 from 41 balls, while Nitish added power and intent with 56 from 33, the pair adding 116 runs for the fifth wicket and lifting the total to a competitive 156 for 9. Prince Yadav and Avesh Khan supported Shami well, combining for four wickets and keeping the run rate in check.

Lucknow’s chase was built on stability rather than speed. Aiden Markram gave early impetus with 45 from 27 balls, taking the score to 77 by the ninth over. Rishabh Pant then anchored the innings with an unbeaten 68 from 50 deliveries, striking nine boundaries and absorbing pressure through the middle. Despite brief stutters that left Lucknow at 139 for 5 in the eighteenth over, Pant held firm to guide the side to 160 for 5 in 19.5 overs..

X FACTOR EXUBERANCE 

Sai Sudharsan has quietly established himself as a silk smooth X factor at the top and through the middle, a left hander whose elegance is matched by ruthless consistency. In 41 matches, he’s piled up an impressive 1,806 runs. He is the very definition of silky consistency and scoreboard pressure. The left hander has quietly grown into a bankable game changer, blending classical strokeplay with modern acceleration. Across 41 matches, Sudarshan has piled up 1,806 runs at an outstanding average of 48.81, striking at a healthy 145.65. Those numbers scream reliability with intent — the kind of batter who anchors an innings yet shifts gears seamlessly when the moment demands.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is a raw talent turned electric impact — a fearless U19 prodigy already announcing himself on the big stage. Also a left hander, Vaibhav plays with breathtaking freedom, taking bowlers on from ball one. In just 8 matches, he has smashed 304 runs at an imposing average of 38, but it’s his jaw?dropping strike rate of 218.71 that truly sets him apart. Blink and you miss it — that’s the tempo he brings. Youthful audacity meets clean hitting, and despite his age, Vaibhav has shown he doesn’t just belong here — he can redefine momentum in a matter of overs.

 

PLAYER BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR 

Shubman Gill vs Jofra Archer
This is a mouth watering contest right at the top of the innings. Shubman Gill, the elegant right hand opening batter, thrives on rhythm and timing, but Jofra Archer brings sheer pace, bounce, and hostility with the new ball. The numbers highlight how fiercely contested this duel has been: Gill has faced 19 deliveries, managing just 16 runs, while Archer has struck 3 times, regularly testing Gill with high pace and hard lengths. In broadcast terms, it’s classic power versus poise — Archer hunting early breakthroughs with raw speed, and Gill looking to weather the storm before unfurling his trademark drives once set.

Shimron Hetmyer vs Rashid Khan
At the death or in the middle overs, this matchup crackles with intent. Shimron Hetmyer, the explosive left handed finisher, loves taking on spin, but Rashid Khan’s world class leg spin has often had the final say. Across 81 balls, Hetmyer has smashed 108 runs, showing his ability to dominate, yet Rashid’s cunning and control are underscored by 7 dismissals — a reminder of how often he has outfoxed the Caribbean dasher. It’s muscle versus mystery: Hetmyer looking to clear the ropes under pressure, and Rashid varying pace and trajectory, betting on skill and deception to turn the game on its head.

 

HEAD TO HEAD IN IPL 

 

VENUE AT A GLANCE

 

MATCH PREVIEW

Match number nine of the TATA IPL 2026 brings the spotlight to Ahmedabad as the Narendra Modi Stadium lights up for the first time this season, with Gujarat Titans squaring off against Rajasthan Royals in a clash rich with recent history. These were the finalists of the 2022 edition, and while circumstances are different now, the edge and expectation remain firmly intact.

Gujarat Titans walk into this contest under pressure, still searching for their first points of the season. Their previous outing against Punjab Kings exposed a fragile middle order, as GT failed to capitalise on promising starts from the top three and were restricted to a sub 170 total in a tight defeat. One shining positive, however, was Prasidh Krishna, whose fiery three wicket burst nearly turned the game on its head. GT will be desperate for a more complete performance tonight as they look to get their campaign up and running.

Rajasthan Royals, in contrast, arrive with confidence flowing. They opened their season with an emphatic eight wicket victory, dismantling Chennai Super Kings with ruthless efficiency. Their bowlers set the tone early, applying relentless pressure, before the chase was lit up by Sooryavanshi, who produced a breathtaking 15 ball fifty, the fastest of IPL 2026 so far. It was a statement win that underlined both intent and depth.

Momentum favours the Royals, urgency fuels the Titans. Ahmedabad promises pace, bounce and big moments, with both sides knowing how quickly seasons can swing. As memories of 2022 resurface and the stakes rise under the lights, the question echoes through the stands. Who takes control tonight, desperation or dominance?
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Ravi Bishnoi (Player of the Match):
"It was a tough season last year and I realised whenever I missed my length I was getting punished, so I focused on tightening that area and accepting the result if batters still hit me. It took mental, technical and physical effort together and I bowled a lot of deliveries to keep improving. My favourite wicket was Rahul Tewatia because he is known to change games in such situations and his dismissal was crucial. If he had stayed, the match could have gone either way. The death bowling from Tushar and Jofra showed how collective the effort was, and as a young team we want to keep entertaining."
 

Riyan Parag (Rajasthan Royals Captain):
"The last two overs were simply incredible and taking the call to bowl full and fast paid off because the boys executed exactly what was planned. I was extremely excited watching them deliver under pressure. I was close to changing the order but Jurel backed Jofra for the nineteenth and that turned out to be a great call. The idea was to bowl as quick and straight as possible at the end and the execution was brilliant. Jurel has done the hard work batting lower down and now at three he has the chance to score big and hopefully lead us to the title."


Rashid Khan (Gujarat Titans Captain):

"We bowled really well overall, especially after the strong start they made, and with the bat too we began nicely before losing back-to-back wickets, which pushed us on the back foot. That phase cost us momentum and on a surface like this scoring nine or ten an over is manageable if you keep the boundaries coming. Losing quick wickets in the middle did not allow us to finish the innings the way we wanted. Everyone has their own game plan but reading conditions and match requirements is crucial and it is still early in the tournament so we will learn and come back stronger. In the final over the talk was about finding one boundary, Tushar executed his yorkers well and I should have taken the chance when he missed, we stay calm, learn from this and hopefully Shubman will be fine for the next game."


Rajasthan Royals held their nerve in a high-scoring encounter at Ahmedabad, completing a tight six-run victory over Gujarat Titans after both sides traded punches through all forty overs in a contest defined by sustained scoring and late tension.

Batting first, Rajasthan posted an imposing 210 for 6, built around a fluent 75 from Dhruv Jurel off 42 balls at a strike rate nearing 180. Yashasvi Jaiswal set the base with 55 from 36 deliveries, putting on 126 for the first two wickets as Rajasthan reached the halfway mark at 113 for 1. Support arrived in bursts, with Shimron Hetmyer striking 18 off 8 and the Royals scoring at over ten an over in 14 of the 20 overs. Gujarat’s bowlers struggled for control, conceding 13 wides, with Rabada finishing with 2 for 42.

Gujarat’s reply stayed competitive throughout. Sai Sudharsan anchored the chase superbly with 73 from 44 balls, bringing up his fifty in just 33 deliveries as Gujarat crossed 100 by the tenth over. A sharp collapse followed as Ravi Bishnoi ripped through the middle order with figures of 4 for 41, removing four batters between overs 12 and 15 to swing momentum Rajasthan’s way. Rashid Khan and Kagiso Rabada mounted a late surge, adding 47 in the final five overs, but the ask proved just beyond reach.

Gujarat closed on 204 for 8, falling short despite scoring at over ten an over in 11 overs, as Rajasthan’s middle overs control proved decisive in a thriller decided by fine margins.

SPOTLIGHT PERFORMERS:
Lungi Ngidi has been in impressive rhythm, as reflected in his T20 WC 2026 numbers—12 wickets from 7 innings, conceding 187 runs in 26 overs at an economy of 7.2 and a strike rate of 13. He also made an immediate impact in the IPL this season, picking up three wickets in the opening game. His ability to strike at crucial moments makes him a key asset for Delhi.

Jasprit Bumrah, meanwhile, has once again showcased his class on the big stage. This T20 WC, in 8 innings, he has claimed 14 wickets, giving away just 174 runs in 28 overs at a superb economy of 6.2, coupled with a strike rate of 12. He also stood out in Mumbai’s opening game, conceding just 35 runs at 8.75 rpo in a high-scoring contest where the opposition piled up 220 at 11 rpo—underlining his exceptional control even under pressure.

 

BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR:
Since 2023, a couple of intriguing match-ups have added an extra layer of context to this contest. Rohit Sharma has scored 22 off 20 deliveries against Axar Patel, with the spinner dismissing him once—making it a finely balanced duel. It will be interesting to see whether Axar continues to operate in the Powerplay to capitalize on this match-up.

Meanwhile, Mukesh Kumar has enjoyed success against Tilak Varma, conceding 26 runs off just 13 deliveries but picking up three crucial dismissals. That battle could once again prove pivotal in shaping the course of the game.

 

HEAD TO HEAD:
Mumbai Indians have held the upper hand in this rivalry in recent seasons, winning four of the last five encounters between the two sides since 2023. Can Delhi shift the balance this time, or will Mumbai continue to ride their momentum?

 

 

MATCH PREVIEW

The first game of the day in the national capital promises a compelling contest as Delhi Capitals take on Mumbai Indians in Match 8 of TATA IPL 2025. Both sides come into this clash riding the momentum of opening wins, setting the stage for an early-season showdown between two confident units.

Delhi Capitals return to familiar territory after a morale-boosting win in Lucknow, where they chased down 142 with 17 balls to spare. What made that victory particularly impressive was their recovery from a precarious 26/4, showcasing both resilience and depth in their batting. Their bowling attack—featuring Lungi Ngidi, T Natarajan, and Kuldeep Yadav—looked sharp and will aim to replicate that dominance at home. Improving their record in Delhi will also be a key focus, especially after managing just one win at this venue last season.

Mumbai Indians, meanwhile, have already ticked off a rare milestone—winning their opening match of the season, something they hadn’t achieved in over a decade. Their batting firepower was on full display as they chased down a formidable 221 against Kolkata, underlining their intent and capability. With confidence high, MI will be keen to build momentum and deliver a complete performance.

Mumbai will aim to keep their pursuit of a long-awaited sixth title on track, while the Axar Patel-led Delhi side sets its sights on improving upon last season’s finish and pushing strongly for a playoff spot this year. With both teams in form and eager to assert early dominance, fans can expect a high-quality contest in Delhi.
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Sameer Rizvi (Player of the Match):

"I have put in a lot of work on myself, especially on areas where I felt I needed improvement like facing fast bowlers. Over the last year I focused on that and also on understanding match situations better. My idea was to take my time, get set and then play my shots rather than rush things. Watching the ball closely and trusting my process helped me, just like it did in the previous game. Scoring runs here gives youngsters like us confidence and opens up more opportunities."


Axar Patel (Delhi Capitals Captain):

"In T20 cricket flexibility is very important and it helps when you have players who can bat in different positions based on the conditions. Promoting or adjusting someone is always a tactical call and it does not guarantee success but it is about reading the day and making the right move. Ashutosh is batting well but selection is always about what the game demands at that moment. Having such options is actually a good problem because it shows our bench strength. The focus is to take one game at a time and maintain momentum, especially when there are gaps between matches."


Suryakumar Yadav (Mumbai Indians Captain):

"It was a decent wicket and we expected it to be on the slower side, and in hindsight we felt we were probably fifteen to twenty runs short of where we wanted to be. I do not want to take anything away from their bowlers and especially Sameer Rizvi who played an outstanding innings and never allowed us back into the game. The pitch stayed pretty similar across both innings and they read the conditions much better and batted beautifully. When batting first there is never a fixed par score but we felt around one eighty to one eighty five would have given us a better chance. Naman and I got out at the wrong time and with the ball there will be days like this so we have to go back to the drawing board and come back better."



Delhi Capitals pulled off a composed chase at the Arun Jaitley Stadium to defeat Mumbai Indians by six wickets, riding on a superb knock from Sameer Rizvi that turned the contest decisively in their favour.
Mumbai Indians never fully broke free after being put in to bat, with early wickets pegging them back before Suryakumar Yadav held the innings together with a fluent fifty, guiding MI to a competitive but subpar 162 for six.
Axar Patel set the tone with a disciplined spell in the powerplay and Mukesh Kumar struck twice early, ensuring Mumbai were kept under pressure throughout their innings despite late cameos from Naman Dhir and Corbin Bosch.

Delhi Capitals stumbled briefly at the top, losing KL Rahul and Nitish Rana cheaply. Still, Rizvi counterattacked magnificently, combining timing with clean power to smash 90 off 51 balls in a match winning performance.
With David Miller providing calm support at the other end, Delhi cruised home in 18.1 overs, sealing two valuable points and underlining their intent early in the IPL 2026 season while Mumbai were left searching for better balance with bat and ball.

SPOTLIGHT PERFORMERS

Ayush Mhatre has burst onto the scene as the fearless U19 sensation who plays with intent from the very first ball. In just 7 matches, he’s smashed 240 runs at a jaw dropping 188.98 strike rate, including a scintillating 94 that showed he can dismantle any attack. His aggressive, top order tempo makes him the quintessential X factor for his side — the kind of explosive opener who can set the tone, seize momentum instantly, and force bowlers into panic mode. He’s young, bold, and built for highlight reel moments.

Prabhsimran Singh, meanwhile, is the seasoned power package for Punjab — a right handed batter who’s stitched together three consistent seasons of impactful cricket. With 549 runs in 17 matches at a blazing 160.53 strike rate and a best of 91, he brings reliability wrapped in six hitting fury. When he gets going, the ball disappears into the stands with effortless timing. Prabhsimran is Punjab’s proven game breaker, the X factor who can shift the rhythm of a match and unleash scoreboard pressure in a heartbeat.

 

 

BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR 

The matchup between Shivam Dube and Arshdeep Singh is a fascinating left hand bat versus left arm pace battle. Arshdeep has been effective against Dube, conceding just 21 runs off 30 balls while dismissing him three times, showing his ability to cramp Dube for room with swing and smart variations. While Dube is known for his power hitting, Arshdeep’s discipline and deft use of angles have often kept the CSK all rounder in check during their encounters.

The contest between Sanju Samson and Yuzvendra Chahal highlights a classic duel of skill and patience. The right handed Samson has scored 49 runs from 59 balls, but Chahal has had the upper hand with five dismissals, underlining the leg spinner’s knack for outthinking aggressive batters. Samson enjoys taking on spinners, yet Chahal’s flight, guile, and ability to induce false shots have repeatedly tilted the balance in his favor, making this a key tactical matchup whenever they face off.


 


HEAD TO HEAD IN IPL 

Since the 2023 IPL season, the head to head battle between Chennai Super Kings (CSK) and Punjab Kings has clearly tilted in Punjab’s favor. Across 5 matches, Punjab Kings have won 4, while CSK have managed just 1 victory, showing Punjab’s recent dominance in this rivalry. Despite CSK’s rich legacy and experience, Punjab have consistently had the upper hand in recent encounters, often outplaying CSK in key moments and making this matchup increasingly competitive and intriguing for fans.




VENUE STATS:

 

MATCH PREVIEW 
Chepauk gets ready to welcome their favourite men in yellow as Chennai Super Kings lock horns with Punjab Kings under the Madras lights. This venue demands craft, control, and serious cricketing IQ where slow burn turns into late over chaos. CSK coming off a loss against Rajasthan Royals in game 01 will look to get back to winning ways at home while Shreyas Iyers men are coming off a comprehensive win against Gujarat Titans . This game has all the pedigree for a blockbuster as both teams pack a punch with their opening combinations . 

CSK will want Ruturaj Gaikwad to come good against the swing of Arshdeep , while Sanju the new fan favourite will look to set the tone with his clean hitting at the start . Punjab Kings will back their pace quadruple of Arshdeep , Bartlett , Jansen & Vyshak as all four can take the pace off and hit the deck hard providing vital breakthroughs at crucial junctures of the game . CSK 's middle order barring Jamie Overton will have to find their feet against the spin of IPL veteran Yuzvendra Chahal . As for Punjab Kings they will look to decimate the home team to continue their winning streak with their young opening combination of Prabhsimran Singh & Priyansh Arya and not to forget the debut dasher Cooper Conolly in the middle order . Anshul Kamboj & Khaleel Ahmed have the ability to generate lateral movement under the lights & Anbu den will be on the edge of their seats wanting to see a tight contest with bat & ball 

Throwback to MS Dhoni's heroics in 2010 , Maxwell's fireworks in 2014 , KL Rahul's sublime hundred in 2020 to last over humdinger in 2023 these two teams have played some intense games over the years in the toughest twenty . Come the 3rd of April Men in Yellow & Red will look to repeat the same brand of bold cricket and fans of the yellow brigade brace yourselves for an  exuberant game of cricket to kick of TATA IPL fever in Chennai.
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Priyansh Arya (Man of the Match) :
“It’s something that’s been part of my game right from the start. I’ve always tried to bat in a way that benefits the team at the end of the day, rather than overthinking the situation.”
“There’s obviously an element of risk involved, but I have a strong belief in my ability. If I feel the first ball is there to be hit, I back myself to go for it.”
“There’s definitely a step up from the DPL to the IPL. At this level, you’re facing far more experienced bowlers, which tests you as a batter and pushes you to improve.”


Shreyas Iyer (Punjab Kings Captain) :
“There’s always a bit of concern before going out to chase a big total, but the way we began today was outstanding. Priyansh and Prabh did a terrific job up front, giving us the perfect platform going into the middle overs. They’ve been batting this way for a long time now, and that consistency really helps settle the rhythm for the rest of us.”
“I’m really pleased that everyone has had the chance to bat within the first couple of games. It builds confidence across the group. When we find ourselves under pressure, people know exactly how to handle the situation and play their roles.”
“There wasn’t anything planned as such. We simply spoke in the dressing room about wanting to impress each other rather than focusing on outside expectations. That mindset has worked well for us.”

Ruturaj Gaikwad (Chennai Super Kings Captain) :
“I honestly felt it was a very good total. The pitch offered plenty for the fast bowlers, while the spinners were getting a bit of grip. Considering the big boundaries at this ground, I thought we had posted a strong score.”
“We already had three seamers in the side, and the idea was to rely on our wrist spinners after the power play. Unfortunately, both of them had a slightly off day, but that was the thinking behind the decision.”
“It was a mix of both execution and timing. We bowled well in patches but failed to do it at the crucial moments. The pressure never truly built. The run rate hovered around 11 — two solid overs could have pushed it to 12 or 13, but we couldn’t maintain sustained pressure.”
“It’s definitely a tough loss, but there are plenty of positives to take. Ayush played an outstanding innings and gave us a lot of confidence going forward. Shivam was impressive as well. As a unit, our bowling was slightly off tonight.”
“I’m feeling good personally. It’s about adapting to what the team requires at any given time. With the pitch gripping a bit and Ayush batting fluently at the other end, I decided to play a supporting role. Unfortunately, I got out at the wrong moment.”

Punjab Kings produced a confident and well paced chase to defeat Chennai Super Kings by five wickets in Match 7 at the MA Chidambaram Stadium, Chennai, on 3 April 2026, successfully overhauling 209 with 8 balls to spare.

After being put in to bat, CSK posted a formidable 209/5 in their 20 overs, built around a dominant middle order performance. Young batter Ayush Mhatre was the standout, hammering 73 off 43 balls, striking six fours and five sixes to anchor the innings after early setbacks. CSK lost Sanju Samson and skipper Ruturaj Gaikwad cheaply, but Mhatre’s 96 run partnership with Gaikwad steadied the innings and laid the platform.

The late surge was powered by Shivam Dube’s unbeaten 45 off 27 balls, complemented by a blistering cameo from Sarfaraz Khan, whose 32 off just 12 deliveries injected crucial momentum in the death overs. CSK added 81 runs in the final five overs, capitalizing on wayward bowling, as Punjab conceded 15 wides overall.

In reply, Punjab Kings paced their chase with composure, keeping the required rate under control despite the pressure of a big total. The batting unit showed excellent intent through the middle and death overs, finding regular boundaries and punishing loose deliveries. Their ability to close out the chase in 18.4 overs underlined a clinical batting performance on a good surface.

For CSK, the bowling struggled to apply consistent pressure, with none of the bowlers managing to stem the flow decisively at the back end. Punjab’s calm approach in crunch moments proved decisive, as they sealed a valuable away victory and important early season momentum.

Toss Update:  won the toss and elected to field first! 

The toss to win pattern refuses to fade in TATA IPL 2026. In the last five matches, every captain winning the toss has chosen to field and chased down targets with comfort, highlighted by DC pulling off a successful chase despite losing four wickets inside the powerplay. So what unfolds tonight? Will someone finally buck the trend, or does the streak roll on? Toss and team details coming up shortly.


PLAYERS TO WATCH OUT:


Ishan Kishan and Finn Allen set up an explosive player battle in the SRH vs KKR clash, based on their World Cup 2026 form. Kishan scored 317 runs in 9 matches at a strike rate of 193.3, with an exceptional 75.7% boundary percentage, showing his ability to combine aggression with consistency. Finn Allen was equally destructive, smashing 298 runs in 8 matches at a staggering strike rate of 200, with 73.8% of his runs in boundaries, highlighting his powerplay dominance. While Allen brings instant impact and chaos, Kishan offers sustained pressure. Whichever batter controls the early overs could define today’s match.
 

BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR:


Several intriguing individual battles could shape the outcome of the KKR vs SRH clash. Sunrisers Hyderabad’s Jaydev Unadkat has dismissed the skipper of Kolkata four times, making him a threat in powerplay. On the other hand, Ishan Kishan has faced Vaibhav Arora just once for 11 runs off 10 balls, but has been dismissed twice, highlighting a potential advantage for the young KKR pacer. These micro-contests, particularly between Klaasen and Narine in the spin-friendly middle overs and Kishan against Arora in the powerplay, could prove decisive in determining which side gains control in a high-scoring encounter.
 

HEAD-TO-HEAD:


Since 2023, Kolkata Knight Riders have had the upper hand against Sunrisers Hyderabad, winning five of their seven encounters, while SRH have managed just two victories. KKR’s dominance in recent seasons highlights their tactical familiarity with SRH, particularly at Eden Gardens, and underscores the psychological advantage they carry going into their matches.
 

VENUE STATS:


Eden Gardens in Kolkata has traditionally favored high-scoring matches, with teams batting first winning seven times and sides chasing prevailing in six encounters. The average first-innings score at the venue is 199, with teams posting around 58 runs in the powerplay and facing a stiff challenge in the death overs, where the run rate climbs to 12.2. The highest successful chase recorded here is 262/2, highlighting the batting-friendly nature of the pitch, especially under lights when dew plays a role. Pace bowlers have been more successful overall, accounting for 68% of wickets, while spinners have claimed the remaining 32%, mainly exploiting the middle overs. Teams batting first aim to set a big total, but chasing sides have an almost equal chance of victory, making strategic planning for toss and bowling rotations crucial at this historically iconic stadium.
 

Kolkata Knight Riders host Sunrisers Hyderabad at Eden Gardens on Thursday, 2 April, in what promises to be a high-scoring encounter. Both teams are looking to recover after their opening games, where they posted 200+ totals but failed to defend, exposing vulnerabilities in their bowling attacks.

KKR, led by Ajinkya Rahane, will play their first home game of the season. Rahane marks his 200th IPL match, a fitting milestone at a venue where KKR have historically dominated SRH. Finn Allen, in sensational form after a 33-ball hundred in the T20 World Cup semi-final at the same ground, is the key threat at the top. The KKR spin duo of Sunil Narine and Varun Chakravarthy is expected to exploit Eden Gardens’ middle overs, historically a spinner-friendly phase. The main concern for KKR remains the absence of frontline pacers Matheesha Pathirana and Cameron Green, who cannot bowl.

SRH, led by Ishan Kishan in Pat Cummins’ absence, also face bowling challenges. Harshal Patel carries the bulk of the pace responsibility, while Jaydev Unadkat and David Payne provide support. Their batting lineup, anchored by Klaasen and Travis Head, will need to fire to counter KKR’s spin threat. Eden Gardens’ dew factor may assist the chasing side, making the toss crucial.

With home advantage, a settled spin duo, and in-form top-order batsmen, KKR are slight favorites, while SRH must rely on big individual performances to turn the tide.
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Nitish Kumar Reddy (Player of the Match):
"It feels really good because this is the match winning performance I had been waiting for over a long time and I am thankful it came at the right moment. I gained a lot of confidence not only with the bat but also with the ball which meant a lot to me. Coming through injuries was mentally tough with many negative thoughts since I could not bowl last season. I worked very hard on my bowling during the break before the IPL and it is satisfying to see that effort paying off on the field. My body is feeling great now and full credit goes to the trainers and physios who have taken excellent care of me."

???????????????????? ???????????????????????????? ???????? ???? pic.twitter.com/1XYvwWY30a

— SunRisers Hyderabad (@SunRisers) April 2, 2026


Ishan Kishan (Sunrisers Hyderabad Captain):
"I was not happy with my own performance but I was really pleased with how the rest of the team committed themselves in the middle and brought great energy which was exactly what I asked for before the game. I did not feel the pitch was very good to bat on but the way Head and Abhishek attacked in the powerplay made things easier and gave everyone time to understand the surface. Credit goes to all our batters especially Klaasen for finishing it off and Nitish for playing a lovely supporting role. Our plan in the second innings was always to hunt wickets because Finn can change games quickly and getting him early helped us. All the bowlers executed the plans really well and that made me very happy as a captain."

 

Ajinkya Rahane (Kolkata Knight Riders Captain):

"I felt we needed one or two big partnerships while chasing because the batters who were set had to take the game deeper. With the ball we did well in the final overs to pull things back, which was a positive for us. In the second innings, the pitch surprised us a bit as the ball held and gripped more than expected but that is no excuse. The middle overs were crucial, and that is where we needed stronger partnerships, while credit goes to the SRH bowlers for using their angles smartly. Moving forward, winning home games is vital, and we need to go back to the drawing board quickly with better awareness and possibly an extra batter. This was just an off day, and we will bounce back."


Sunrisers Hyderabad delivered one of their most dominant batting performances of the season at Eden Gardens, piling up a formidable 226 for 8 and leaving Kolkata Knight Riders with far too much to chase under sustained pressure. The innings was powered by brutal intent at the top, with Travis Head smashing 46 off just 21 balls and Abhishek Sharma matching him stroke for stroke with 48 from 21 as Hyderabad raced to 82 inside the first 5.4 overs.

The momentum did not slow once the early wickets fell. Heinrich Klaasen anchored the middle phase with a commanding 52 off 35 balls, striking at nearly 150, while Nitish Kumar Reddy supplied late acceleration with 39 from 24 to push the run rate above eleven. Hyderabad scored at over ten runs an over in 15 of the 20 overs, underlining the relentlessness of their approach, even as Blessing Muzarabani finished with four wickets amid the damage.

Kolkata’s reply began in whirlwind fashion with Finn Allen blasting 28 off seven balls, but the chase quickly lost shape as wickets fell in clusters. Angkrish Raghuvanshi stood firm with a fighting 52 from 29, yet with the asking rate above fourteen, pressure mounted relentlessly.

Jaydev Unadkat’s three for 21 and disciplined support from the rest ensured Kolkata were bowled out for 161 in just 16 overs. The result was decided by Hyderabad’s sustained scoring power, turning a competitive contest into a one sided spectacle marked by sheer batting supremacy.

LANDMARKS ON THE LINE

 

VENUE AT A GLANCE
Ekana Stadium has leaned heavily towards chasing sides in recent times. Across the 15 IPL matches played here since 2024, teams batting second have dominated with 10 wins, while those setting a target have managed just five victories. The average first innings score is a formidable 189, with powerplays producing around 57 runs. Death overs have offered little relief to bowlers, who have conceded at 10.5 runs an over. Pace bowlers have enjoyed greater success, accounting for 70 percent of the wickets, while spinners have chipped in with the remaining 30 percent.



HEAD TO HEAD IN IPL
Delhi Capitals hold a narrow advantage over Lucknow Super Giants in their IPL rivalry, edging ahead with four wins compared to LSG’s three across seven encounters. With such fine margins separating the two sides, this matchup remains delicately balanced and primed for another closely fought contest.

 

PLAYER BATTLES TO WATCH OUT FOR 


Fiery versus clever, this is a showdown built for prime-time drama. Rishabh Pant’s instinctive, no fear approach clashes head on with Kuldeep Yadav’s deceptive left arm wizardry, a duel that’s swung on subtle margins. Across five innings, Pant scores at a strike rate of 92, but Kuldeep has had the last laugh three times, slipping past the bat with variation and dip. Then comes the classic contest of poise against precision. KL Rahul’s silken timing and control power a strike rate of 122.85 in five innings, yet Mohammed Shami’s relentless back of a length accuracy and seam movement have accounted for him twice. These aren’t just numbers, they’re pressure points. Two battles, four elite operators, and match defining moments ready to explode.

 

 

PROBABLE PLAYING XI :

LUCKNOW SUPER GIANTS : A Markram, M Marsh, N Pooran, R Pant (C/Wk), Breetzke, A Badoni, A Samad, S Ahmed. M Khan, A Khan, M Shami , Digvesh Rathi (Impact Sub)

 

DELHI CAPITALS : KL Rahul (Wk), A Porel / P Nissanka , N Rana, A Patel (C), T Stubbs, D Miller , A Sharma , V Nigam, K Yadav, L Ngidi, M Kumar, T Natarajan (Impact Sub)

 

STRATEGIC TRUMPCARDS TO WATCH OUT 

Ashutosh Sharma headlines the X factor narrative with ice cold finishing pedigree and proven big moment intent. The right hander stamped authority last season against this very opposition, sealing the game in the final over with clean hitting and fearless shot selection. His numbers underline the threat, 393 runs in 24 matches at a blistering strike rate of 163.75, including a career best 66*. Competing against him is Digvesh Rathi, the unconventional leg spinner who bends norms and conversations alike. In just 13 matches, Rathi has picked up 14 wickets at a strike rate of 22.29, maintaining pressure with an economy of 8.25. Flashy celebrations aside, his impact is real, one closer, one disruptor, both ready to tilt momentum.

 


Lucknow is locked in and loud as Match 05 of TATA IPL 2026 brings back a spicy rivalry under the Ekana lights. Lucknow Super Giants take on Delhi Capitals in a clash that already has history written all over it. For context, rewind to IPL 2025’s opening clash for these two sides, a certified humdinger where Delhi snatched victory from the jaws of defeat, thanks to Ashutosh Sharma’s ice cold last over finish and some clutch support from Vipraj Nigam. That game still lives rent free in fans’ heads, and tonight feels like unfinished business.

LSG are pulling up stacked, especially in the pace department. The return of Mohammed Shami brings old school control and big game experience, while left arm angle merchant Mohsin Khan adds variety. And then there’s Mayank Yadav, the human speedgun. If Ekana offers its usual two paced bounce early on, this trio could straight up make life uncomfortable for Delhi’s top order.But don’t sleep on the Capitals. The big headline  David Miller in Delhi colours, turning up against his former franchise. The man’s clutch gene is elite, and in pressure chases, he’s still him. Pair that with Delhi’s fearless middle order, and you’ve got a unit that thrives when chaos hits.

Ekana usually rewards discipline and smart match ups, so expect captains to play chess, not checkers. With emotion, history, and serious firepower on both sides, this one’s giving high voltage vibes. Fans, lock in, this could be cinema again.
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Sameer Rizvi (Player of the Match):
"I was told clearly during practice that I would bat at number four and the coaches gave me a lot of backing which helped my confidence. Playing at home made a difference because I know these conditions well from my state league cricket. I wanted to be positive early but once two wickets fell the situation changed and Stubbs reminded me to respect the conditions first. After getting set we knew we could play our natural game without rushing. It was never about reaching a fifty but about taking on the bad balls and putting them away when the opportunity came."

— Delhi Capitals (@DelhiCapitals) April 1, 2026


Axar Patel (DC Captain):
"The game finished early but we came close in the middle and the conversation with the bat was always about building one big partnership before finishing strongly. With the new ball doing a bit we were very happy with how our bowlers responded and executed the plans we discussed. Knowing the pitch was helping the pacers and with a big boundary I felt it made sense to use them more and bowl myself less. The plan was to introduce Rizvi early while keeping Ashutosh Sharma as an option if the innings started well. Ashutosh showed last year what he can do and this time Rizvi stepped up, he has been excellent in practice and it was good to see him pull it off tonight."


Rishabh Pant (LSG Captain):
"Ideal way to move on from a run out is to ignore it because dwelling on it only puts you on the back foot and there is nothing you can change once it has happened. With limited bowling options we were forced into using a batter because we could not build partnerships or put enough runs on a good surface. The batting order call was about left right combinations and giving Badoni responsibility in tough situations even though it did not come off this time. There was some help with the new ball but with only one hundred and forty runs it is hard to put the opposition under pressure despite the boys fighting hard. Extra runs always hurt and for the rest of the season you will definitely see me continue batting in the top order even if it remains a fifty fifty call."


Delhi Capitals scripted a calm and controlled chase at the Ekana Stadium, overcoming early damage to defeat Lucknow Super Giants by six wickets with 17 balls to spare.

Lucknow were bundled out for 141 in 18.4 overs after never quite settling with the bat. Mitchell Marsh top scored with 35 from 28 balls, while Abdul Samad added late resistance with 36 off 25, but wickets fell at regular intervals. Lucknow slipped from 65 for 4 in the ninth over to 105 for 6 by the thirteenth, losing momentum completely. Delhi’s bowling unit worked in tandem, led by T Natarajan who claimed 3 for 29, while Kuldeep Yadav and Lungi Ngidi chipped in with five wickets between them to keep the scoring rate under eight an over.

The chase appeared shaky at first as Delhi slumped to 26 for 4 inside five overs, including a first ball dismissal for KL Rahul. From there, Sameer Rizvi assembled a match defining partnership with Tristan Stubbs, adding an unbroken 119 for the fifth wicket. Rizvi anchored the innings with a composed 70 off 47 balls, striking at nearly 149, while Stubbs supported with 39 from 32.

Delhi reached 145 for 4 in 17.1 overs, sealing a victory built on recovery, patience and a decisive middle order stand that swung the game firmly in their favour.

Pitch Report by Pommie Mbangwa and Michael Clarke:

"The conditions feel outstanding and this is a fantastic stadium with a smooth outfield right up to the boundary, and while we will get to the pitch shortly it already looks a good one. In terms of dimensions, one side is sixty four metres, the other around seventy, straight is seventy four with a seventy five metre pocket, which means you have to hit cleanly or run hard in those areas. Michael Clarke feels this is a beautiful surface with a rock hard strip underneath, slightly dry grass and expects minimal spin with no major change whether teams bat or bowl first. This looks like a genuine high-scoring venue with Clarke eyeing totals above two hundred and the average batting first here sitting around two one seven. With little dew expected, teams can choose either option since some prefer chasing while others like to set a total."



Players to Watch
For Punjab Kings, Prabhsimran Singh remains a vital spark at the top. His explosive starts have often tilted games early, and the numbers underline his impact. Across 51 IPL innings, he has piled up 1305 runs at a striking rate of 152, registering eight scores over fifty, including seven half-centuries and a memorable century. When he fires, PBKS tend to follow.

Gujarat Titans, meanwhile, draw assurance from the consistency of Sai Sudharsan. Reliability has been his trademark, with 1793 runs amassed from just 40 matches. Twelve fifties and two hundreds speak of his appetite for big scores, all delivered at a healthy strike rate of 146. For GT, his presence at the crease often forms the backbone of their batting effort.



Venue at a Glance
Since 2024, the New Chandigarh venue has hosted 11 IPL matches and the numbers suggest a fine balance with a slight tilt towards teams batting first. Sides setting a target have won six games, while chasing teams have prevailed five times. Pace bowlers have enjoyed greater influence here, claiming 67 percent of the wickets, with spinners accounting for the remaining 33 percent. The powerplay has offered steady scoring opportunities, averaging around 53 runs, while the death overs have been tough on bowlers, who have gone at 10.6 an over. The average first innings score at this venue stands at 169, keeping contests competitive and tightly poised.



Head-to-Head in IPL
Punjab Kings and Gujarat Titans are locked in a perfectly balanced rivalry so far. In the six IPL encounters between the two sides, both teams have claimed three victories each, making it an even-steven contest heading into their next clash.




Punjab Kings and Gujarat Titans are set for an intriguing clash as two contrasting styles go head-to-head in the TATA IPL 2026. While PBKS thrive on explosive starts, GT prefer building a solid base before accelerating — a classic fire vs ice contest.

Punjab come into this game with continuity and confidence after narrowly missing the title last season. Their approach remains aggressive at the top, with Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh expected to take the attack to the opposition early. With a strong middle order featuring Shreyas Iyer, Marcus Stoinis and Nehal Wadhera, PBKS also have the depth to sustain momentum through the innings. Their bowling unit, led by Arshdeep Singh, Marco Jansen and Yuzvendra Chahal, offers both pace and spin variety.

Gujarat Titans, on the other hand, rely heavily on their consistent opening pair of Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan, who were among the most productive duos last season. Their ability to anchor the innings allows the middle order to play defined roles, though that remains an area GT will want more output from. With experienced names like Jos Buttler, Rahul Tewatia and Washington Sundar, they have the tools but need better consistency. Their bowling attack looks formidable, with Mohammed Siraj, Rashid Khan and Kagiso Rabada forming a strong core.

The pitch at Mullanpur is expected to offer a balanced contest. While big scores are possible, totals around 180-190 have often been competitive, bringing both batters and bowlers into play.

Ultimately, the match could be decided in the PowerPlay. If PBKS get their explosive start, they could dictate terms early. But if GT’s openers settle in, they have the structure to control the game. A tight contest awaits.
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Cooper Connolly (Player of the Match):
"I wanted to focus on gaining experience by playing good cricket alongside world-class players, and it was nice to put on a show and start the season with a win. Before this game, I had a small reset with a couple of practice days at home and time with family and friends, which helped me come in feeling fresh. I see myself as more of a tempo player rather than someone who goes for constant big hits, and working with Ricky has been excellent in helping me find that rhythm early. When things got tight in the chase, I stayed relaxed and felt we were always in a good position with Marco and Xavier calm as well, which helped the dressing room. Facing Rashid for the first time was a great contest and contributing for the team makes me excited for the rest of the tournament."


 

THE N?E?X?T? BIG THING ??????#CooperConnolly pic.twitter.com/lOWAlCLYF9

— Punjab Kings (@PunjabKingsIPL) March 31, 2026

Shreyas Iyer (Punjab Kings Captain):
"My hand feels fine and pretty much the same as before, so I hope it stays that way and everything settles well. Cooper Connolly was outstanding and some of the shots he played were unbelievable, especially the clean backfoot six off Rashid which showed his confidence and ability. In the dugout I was icing my hand when we suddenly lost two wickets, but moments like that are part of the IPL and staying calm is important, especially when someone as composed as Cooper is set at the crease. Vyshak has carried forward his form from last season, he thrives in big moments, and seeing captains trying aggressive fields like forward short leg is refreshing and good for the game."


 

Shubman Gill (Gujarat Titans Captain):
"I felt we bowled quite well overall but the last five or six overs did not go our way, and while batting we knew this was not a two hundred wicket with the talk in the middle being around one seventy or one eighty. The chase felt gettable at one stage but we kept losing wickets and did not bat through the final overs which made the difference. As the ball aged it became harder to hit straight and there was not much pace from the surface, while in the second innings the wet ball from rain meant it did not grip as much and I thought we still did a decent job with the runs available. Prasidh came in at the right time because we felt a skiddy quick could make an impact on a slow pitch and he did his role well, while Ashok keeps working hard and hopefully shows his skills consistently with the new ball. The message to the boys is simple, it was tough luck today but keep the intensity high and continue giving your best."


 

Punjab Kings emerged victorious after a tense and fluctuating contest, edging past Gujarat Titans by three wickets in a match where momentum refused to settle for long and scoring rates stayed relentlessly high.

Gujarat Titans put up 162 for 6, a total built steadily rather than spectacularly. Shubman Gill’s 39 off 27 set the platform, Jos Buttler added 38 from 33, and Glenn Phillips kept the innings moving with 25 from 17. Punjab’s bowlers prevented any late surge, led by Vijaykumar Vyshak’s 3 for 34 and Yuzvendra Chahal’s economical 2 for 28, restricting Gujarat to just 43 runs in the final five overs.

Punjab’s chase began with clear intent and control. By the 14th over, they were firmly ahead, riding on Cooper Connolly’s debut knock and Prabhsimran Singh’s 37, with the pair adding 76 and keeping the asking rate manageable. At that stage, Punjab looked in command.

The match turned sharply in the 15th over when Prasidh Krishna struck twice, dragging Gujarat back into the contest and swinging the win probability from around 30 percent to nearly 52 percent. Just as pressure peaked, Marco Jansen and Connolly responded with authority, reclaiming momentum and calming the chase.

From there on, Punjab did not look back. Connolly remained unbeaten on 72 off 44, guiding the side home in 19.1 overs. With both teams scoring at over nine a piece for much of the game, it was a thriller shaped by sharp momentum shifts and decisive individual moments.

Head-to-Head in IPL:

This rivalry has been as close as it gets. Across 31 IPL meetings, Chennai Super Kings hold a slender edge with 16 victories, while Rajasthan Royals are right behind with 15 wins. The numbers reflect just how evenly matched these two sides have been over the years, setting the stage for yet another tight contest.



Match number three of the TATA IPL 2026 injects tradition and anticipation into the early phase of the tournament as Rajasthan Royals take on Chennai Super Kings in a contest steeped in legacy at the Barsapara Cricket Stadium. These were the two sides that contested the very first IPL final back in 2008, and as the league enters its 19th edition, the reunion arrives with new faces, new leadership strands and renewed urgency, even though it marks just the opening fixture of the season for both teams.

Both franchises come into this clash driven by correction and clarity after enduring a tough campaign last year, finishing in the bottom two. Squads have been reshaped, combinations refined and identities redefined. Rajasthan Royals look lighter, sharper and more flexible, while Chennai Super Kings arrive with their familiar blend of calm experience and fearless youth, determined to reassert their standard early.
One of the strongest subplots surrounds Sanju Samson, the former Royals captain, now lining up against his old franchise in yellow. Few know the opposition better, and that emotional edge could matter in tight moments. Equally intriguing is the youth battle between Mhatre and Suryavanshi, two exciting young prospects eager to seize their opportunity and announce themselves on a big stage.

Barsapara is ready for a vibrant evening, with energy in the stands and intent in the middle. Both teams understand the value of a strong first impression in a long tournament. History looms, hunger burns and belief waits to be validated.

Two champions, one fresh beginning. When the first ball is bowled, someone will set the tone early. Who draws first blood in IPL 2026?
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Nandre Burger (Player of the Match):
"It feels really good to contribute early and help the team get off to a strong start. Bowling first was ideal for us and we had watched games here and prepared knowing conditions would assist the bowlers. Against Samson the plan was simple because every batter is a wicket and it comes down to using natural variation and forcing shots into areas we want. The dressing room feels the same as before with the same coaching staff and familiar environment. It is nice to see everyone smiling and starting the tournament on a positive note."
 

Nandre andar, dono openers bahar ???????? pic.twitter.com/IVs26vf4pJ

— Rajasthan Royals (@rajasthanroyals) March 30, 2026

Riyan Parag (Rajasthan Royals Captain):
"I felt the toss went our way and the execution that followed was excellent, especially after spending a few days practicing here before CSK arrived. Knowing it was a red soil pitch and that rain had added some moisture helped us plan better and everyone carried out their roles really well. Being the full time captain feels right because a lot of preparation and planning has gone into this team and I am grateful to the franchise for trusting me. The goal for us this season is very clear and that is to win the trophy after putting in a lot of work behind the scenes. We want to play smart cricket, adapt quickly to conditions and today was a good example of doing exactly that."
 

Ruturaj Gaikwad (Chennai Super Kings Captain):
"I did not feel overly frustrated because the conditions early on were difficult with the quicks bowling well and the spinners also getting help, so it was always going to be a tough surface to bat on. Our idea was to take the game deep and show calm batsmanship to push the score towards one hundred and fifty or one hundred and sixty, but in today’s game it is hard to know what a safe total really is. From here the focus is to move on quickly because we have another match in three days and everyone must stay in the right mental space. It helps that the group is young and eager, and on another day the same players can step up and deliver strong performances. With the impact player rule you have to stay positive while batting first and back your instincts because if the ball is there to be hit you must take that option."


Rajasthan Royals  combined sharp bowling figures with ruthless batting numbers to register a dominant win over Chennai Super Kings  at Guwahati, closing the chase in just 12.1 overs and underlining their control through every phase of the match.


Chennai Super Kings were restricted to 127 all out in 19.4 overs, a total shaped by early collapses that left them 41 for 4 inside the powerplay. Jofra Archer led the charge with figures of 4 overs for 19 runs and 2 wickets, while Nandre Burger matched him strike for strike, conceding only 26 across his spell. Ravindra Jadeja added control in the middle overs, picking up 2 for 18 as Chennai slipped to 74 for 7 by the 11th over. Jamie Overton top scored with 43 from 36 balls, accounting for more than a third of the total, but support remained limited.


The chase was effectively decided in the first six overs. Rajasthan raced to 74 without loss in the powerplay, driven by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s explosive 52 off just 17 balls, struck at a staggering rate above 300. Yashasvi Jaiswal anchored the innings with an unbeaten 38 from 36 deliveries, ensuring stability as the target approached. The Royals crossed the line at 128 for 2, finishing with 47 balls remaining and sealing a win defined by numbers as much as authority.

Pitch Report by Pommie Mbangwa and Michael Clarke

Standing at the iconic Wankhede Stadium, the first thing that stands out is the inviting boundary size, roughly 64 metres square on both sides and about 75 metres straight, a venue where IPL games regularly see an average of 14 sixes clearing the ropes. The surface itself looks in excellent shape, carrying a hint of grass that is more than usual for Mumbai. That covering should help the pitch stay firm and true, offering good pace and bounce, especially with the new ball. Seamers who hit the deck hard and mix their lengths are likely to be rewarded, while smart use of slower balls and pinpoint yorkers will be vital as the innings progresses. Traditionally, teams here prefer bowling first, backing themselves to chase with the aid of dew later in the night. If a side is batting first, a score around the 200 mark feels like the benchmark needed to feel safe on a ground that rarely forgives anything less.



IPL Stats at Wankhede Stadium since 2024

In the 14 matches played at the Wankhede Stadium since 2024, the venue has offered an even contest between bat and ball. Teams batting first and chasing have enjoyed identical success, with seven wins each. The powerplay has been productive for batters, yielding an average of 54 runs, while the mean first innings total stands at 182. Bowlers have found little respite at the death, conceding close to 11 runs per over. Pacers continue to play a decisive role, picking up 74 percent of the wickets, with spinners accounting for the remaining 26 percent through control and pressure.



Head-to-Head in IPL:

Mumbai Indians have traditionally dominated this rivalry, winning 24 of the 35 IPL matches played against Kolkata Knight Riders, while KKR have managed 11 victories. However, context adds intrigue to the numbers. When it comes to season openers, Mumbai Indians carry an unwanted streak, having lost their first match of every IPL season since 2012. Dominance in the rivalry meets an opening day jinx tonight, raising a compelling question under the Wankhede lights. Will history repeat itself once more, or can MI finally break the shackles and start IPL 2026 on a winning note?



Hello and welcome to our live coverage of match two of the TATA IPL 2026 as the Mumbai Indians host the Kolkata Knight Riders at the iconic Wankhede Stadium. Both sides are eager to kick-start their campaigns with a win. Toss updates, team news and some interesting facts coming your way shortly.

Match number two of TATA IPL 2026 lights up the Wankhede Stadium as the Mumbai Indians  welcome the Kolkata Knight Riders  in a collision of pedigree, power and purpose. Two of the league’s most consistent franchises square off early, and when blue meets purple under Mumbai lights, intensity is never in short supply. This one feels loaded even before the first ball is bowled.


Kolkata Knight Riders arrive with a few injury clouds hovering, but their spin bowling spine looks settled and dangerous. Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine remain the twin pillars, capable of strangling innings and shifting tempo quietly. The addition of Cameron Green adds muscle and versatility, and there is an extra narrative there, returning to a venue where he once wore Mumbai colours and left an impression. KKR may be patched up in places, but tactically, they look ready.


Mumbai Indians, meanwhile, appear stacked with confidence and clarity. Their Indian core is humming, led by Hardik Pandya, whose presence alone sharpens belief. With Sherfane Rutherford adding firepower, Jack’s recent form injecting momentum, and Trent Boult’s calm, swing and big match experience, MI seem well prepared to exploit familiar conditions. Wankhede has always rewarded brave batting and smart pace bowling, areas Mumbai traditionally dominate.


This is not just an early-season fixture; it is a tone setter. The crowd, the lights, the rivalry and the firepower promise drama from start to finish. Two heavyweights, two strong identities, one result. As the sea breeze rolls in and stakes rise, the question hangs heavy over Wankhede: Who takes the first big step forward in the TATA IPL 2026?
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Shardul Thakur (Player of the Match):
"I enjoy challenges and this was a tough situation especially that sixth over when early wickets did not come which made it testing to run in and execute. This ground has given me highs and lows over the years and playing here for Mumbai Indians at my home venue feels extremely special. I started here quietly as a net bowler and to represent this franchise now feels like something that was always meant to happen. The backing I have received since being traded here and playing the first game means a lot to me and I would love to stay here for my career. As for the Lord tag it comes with love from the fans and I see it as affection more than anything else."

???????????????? on his MI debut, Lord Shardul has arrived and how! ????#MIvKKR pic.twitter.com/JAQil0reCj

— Mumbai Indians (@mipaltan) March 29, 2026


Hardik Pandya (Mumbai Indians Captain):
"It has been a long wait to finally start the season with a win and the entire group is delighted that we managed to get that done. In modern cricket targets around two hundred and twenty are chaseable especially on wickets like this and our bowlers deserve credit for ensuring KKR did not run away to much bigger totals. Rohit and Rickelton were outstanding at the top and whenever Rohit plays those shots it leaves everyone in awe. When it came to choosing between Rickelton and Quinny we backed continuity and Ryan earned that trust while Quinny understood the decision which helped team balance. I told Shardul that he belongs here for the long run because of how cleverly he bowls and outsmarts batters which was brilliant to watch tonight."

Ajinkya Rahane (Kolkata Knight Riders Captain)
"Feeling fine overall, although I did have quite a few cramps especially around my calves and hopefully that settles down soon. It was tough on the bowlers with a relatively inexperienced attack but this is part of the learning curve and they will improve with time. The opposition batted really well and that kind of challenge will only help our bowlers grow. I am still feeling good with my batting and enjoying my cricket, and once Green starts bowling our team balance will look different. There were positives like Kartik Tyagi bowling well after a long gap and we know there is a long road ahead in the tournament."


Mumbai Indians pulled off a spirited chase at the Wankhede, prevailing over Kolkata Knight Riders in a contest that steadily rose into a high-scoring spectacle and ended with the home side asserting composure under pressure.

Kolkata Knight Riders had earlier produced a commanding total of 220, built on a captain’s innings from Ajinkya Rahane who anchored the effort with a composed sixty seven while Finn Allen provided early momentum with crisp powerplay hitting. Angkrish Raghuvanshi added flair through the middle overs and Rinku Singh finished with authority, ensuring Kolkata maintained a relentless scoring rate despite regular wickets.

Mumbai’s reply was defined by a blistering opening stand that quickly altered the tone of the chase. Rohit Sharma played with freedom and ferocity, racing to seventy eight, while Ryan Rickelton matched him stroke for stroke with a superb eighty one that blended brute strength with clean timing. The pair erased any early advantage Kolkata had created and pushed the asking rate firmly into Mumbai’s comfort zone.

Mumbai never lost control. Hardik Pandya held his nerve late on and guided the chase home with calm assurance, sealing a victory that highlighted Mumbai’s batting depth and their ability to thrive in pressure driven pursuits.

Pitch Report by Morrison and Pietersen:
With a touch of cloud cover hovering but conditions largely ideal, the M Chinnaswamy Stadium once again sets up as a paradise for batters. The boundaries are compact, around 64 to 65 metres square and just 73 metres straight, offering little breathing space for bowlers. The surface looks good for stroke play, and while a few bare areas can be spotted, they are not expected to alter proceedings significantly. Considering the strength, bat speed and modern power games of today’s players, this ground feels even smaller. Once batters settle in, runs should flow freely, and with conditions holding steady, winning the toss and opting to chase could be the smart call tonight.



The Big Battle to Watch Out For:

Virat Kohli versus Harshal Patel could well shape the tone of the evening. Across six innings, Kohli has muscled 80 runs from just 47 balls at a strike rate north of 170, but Harshal has had his say too, dismissing the talisman twice when it mattered.


Hello and welcome to our live coverage of the IPL 2026 opener as the two sides from the unforgettable 2016 final begin their new campaigns tonight. Toss updates, pitch reports, and team news are on the way. Until then, soak in our match preview below.

The curtains rise on TATA IPL 2026 with a fixture soaked in story and sound as Royal Challengers Bengaluru  begin their title defence against familiar headaches Sunrisers Hyderabad  at M Chinnaswamy Stadium on 28 March. and the champions know that opening nights can define moods long before they define tables. Yet every RCB fan also knows that when SRH arrive, history refuses to stay silent.


Bengaluru’s build up carries pride tempered with concern as Josh Hazelwood continues his recovery, pushing Rajat Patidar and his men toward early adaptability. All eyes turn to the opening pair of Virat Kohli and Phil Salt, a combination carved for velocity and intent on short boundaries. A fast start is not a luxury here, it is a requirement, with the middle order itching to turn belief into dominance in front of their own people.


Sunrisers Hyderabad land with a refreshed squad and an intriguing leadership subplot. Ishan Kishan steps in as captain with Pat Cummins set to join later, bringing urgency and aggression to the role. An Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head opening burst threatens to hush crowds quickly, and SRH have shown before that Bengaluru’s noise fuels them rather than frightens them.


The Harshal Patel and Bhuvneshwar Kumar duel adds poetic spice to the night. Harshal won his Purple Cap wearing RCB colours and now returns in orange aiming to haunt his former home, while Bhuvneshwar, once SRH’s Purple Cap hero, now wears red as he looks to undo the side he once led with the ball. Experience, movement and nerve will matter most under lights.


Season openers thrive on chaos and courage. Champions against challengers,  belief against memory. Somewhere between a roaring crowd and a swinging ball, IPL 2026 will announce itself, loudly and without apology.
 

Head to Head In IPL:

History slightly favours the men in orange whenever these two sides collide. Across 25 fiercely fought IPL encounters, Sunrisers Hyderabad have emerged victorious 14 times, a tally that includes one dramatic Super Over finish. Royal Challengers Bengaluru follow closely with 11 wins, proof that margins have often been slim. While RCB now carry champion swagger, past numbers whisper that SRH know how to make this rivalry uncomfortable, especially when pressure is highest.



 

Venue at a Glance

M Chinnaswamy Stadium continues to live up to its reputation as a balanced yet explosive venue. Over the last two seasons, 12 matches have been played here with teams batting first and second winning an equal six games each, underlining the absence of a clear toss advantage.

The average first innings score stands at a healthy 186, driven largely by frenetic powerplays that have produced an average of 56 runs. Once the game slips into the final overs, bowlers have found little mercy, conceding at a sharp run rate of 11.4 in the death phase.
Fast bowlers have still found a way to influence proceedings, accounting for 75 percent of the wickets, while spinners have chipped in with the remaining 25 percent, usually when batters gamble for glory.
Matchsummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Jacob Duffy (Player of the Match):
"It was a great experience playing my first game here because I had heard so much about RCB and it was quite a way to begin the campaign. I was just keeping the big fella’s seat warm and felt lucky that the captain kept trusting me with the ball which meant my work finished early. Bowling to Ishan Kishan and Abhishek is never easy and this surface is not very friendly for bowlers but I am learning quickly. Rajat simply told me to go ahead and bowl when the ball was hard because that is the best time to make an impact. Overall it was a special start and I really enjoyed the challenge."
 

Flying start to our very own Kiwi, Jacob Duffy. ?????????

POTM on his IPL debut. ??

— Royal Challengers Bengaluru (@RCBTweets) March 28, 2026

Rajat Patidar (Royal Challengers Bengaluru Captain):
"It was a perfect start to the season and I am very happy with the way the boys performed in the opening game. Duffy was outstanding in his first match and picking up key wickets early made a big difference while Abhinandan also impressed on debut. There are no words for the way our main batter keeps finishing games because he is consistently there till the end and makes things happen. The catch from Phil Salt was terrific and he is an exceptional fielder who produces moments like that regularly. I tried to add as much impact as possible with the bat and that intent will stay throughout the season."

 

Ishan Kishan (Sunrisers Hyderabad Captain):
"The wicket settled down after the first few overs but losing early wickets meant we had to be careful and assess things better. It played even better in the second innings so we need to be smarter with our shot selection going forward. Credit has to go to the opposition batters, especially Virat bhai, because getting his wicket early is always crucial. These mistakes are understandable in the first game, but we need to learn quickly and improve as a bowling unit. I really enjoyed the captaincy experience and while there are nerves in the IPL if we keep winning games it will set us up well for the rest of the tournament."



Royal Challengers Bengaluru  produced a statement chase to outclass Sunrisers Hyderabad , transforming a demanding target into an exhibition of control, confidence and depth.

Sunrisers Hyderabad had earlier leaned heavily on Ishan Kishan, whose commanding eighty off thirty-eight balls rescued the innings after early setbacks. Kishan dictated the middle overs with authority, combining clean striking with smart placement as wickets fell around him. Aniket Verma then provided a late surge with a ferocious forty three, ensuring Hyderabad crossed the two-hundred mark and kept the contest alive.

Bengaluru’s response was swift and decisive. Devdutt Padikkal set the tone with an elegant yet aggressive sixty one off twenty six deliveries, tearing into the powerplay and shifting momentum permanently. His timing was pure and his intent unmistakable, forcing the bowlers off their plans.

Virat Kohli followed with a composed unbeaten sixty nine, anchoring the chase with experience and assurance while allowing others to play freely around him. Rajat Patidar contributed a sharp and impactful cameo, and Tim David calmly applied the finishing touches.

The target was reached with ease and time to spare, as Bengaluru delivered a performance that stood as a confident reminder of their batting strength rather than merely an afterthought to Hyderabad’s effort.
